language: class Solution { // Function to find the median of two sorted arrays medianOf2(a, b) { let n = a.length; // Ensure `a` is the smaller array for optimized binary search // Time Complexity: O(log(min(n, m))) - where n and m are the lengths of a and b if (n > b.length) return this.medianOf2(b, a); let low = 0, high = n; // Binary search to find the correct partition while (low <= high) { let partitionA = Math.floor((low + high) / 2); let partitionB = n - partitionA; // Left and right boundaries for both partitions let leftA = partitionA === 0 ? -Infinity : a[partitionA - 1]; // Max left of A let rightA = partitionA === n ? Infinity : a[partitionA]; // Min right of A let leftB = partitionB === 0 ? -Infinity : b[partitionB - 1]; // Max left of B let rightB = partitionB === n ? Infinity : b[partitionB]; // Min right of B // If valid partition is found, calculate the median if (leftA <= rightB && leftB <= rightA) { // Median is the average of max of lefts and min of rights for even-sized array // Time Complexity for this step is O(1) since we only compare 4 elements return (Math.max(leftA, leftB) + Math.min(rightA, rightB)) / 2; } // If leftA is too large, move partitionA left else if (leftA > rightB) { high = partitionA - 1; } // If leftB is too large, move partitionA right else { low = partitionA + 1; } } return -1; // Should never reach here if arrays are valid } }
problemTitle: Median of 2 Sorted Arrays of Same Size
